title: Lesson Quiz: What Is Government?
description: Assessment of the concept that The purpose of government, maintaining order, and providing public services.

questions.prompt: Which statement best explains the main purpose of government?

choices.text: To remove all rules from society
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Government exists to create structure, not remove all rules.

choices.text: To allow every person to make separate laws
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Government creates shared laws for society rather than separate laws for each person.

choices.text: To maintain order, provide services, and make decisions for society
choices.feedback: Correct. This matches the lesson focus on order, services, and decision-making.

choices.text: To prevent communities from working together
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Government helps organize society and support cooperation.

questions.prompt: Which example shows government providing a public service?

choices.text: A student chooses a favorite hobby
choices.feedback: Incorrect. A personal hobby is not a government service.

choices.text: A city maintains roads and transportation systems
choices.feedback: Correct. Roads and transportation are examples of public services.

choices.text: A family decides what to cook for dinner
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is a private household decision, not a public service.

choices.text: A friend sends a text message
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Sending a text is not a government service.

questions.prompt: How does government help maintain order?

choices.text: By creating and enforcing rules
choices.feedback: Correct. The lesson explains that governments maintain order through rules and laws.

choices.text: By removing all laws and expectations
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Removing laws would reduce order, not maintain it.

choices.text: By encouraging confusion in communities
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Government is meant to organize society, not confuse it.

choices.text: By preventing all decisions from being made
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments make decisions to help society function.

questions.prompt: Why would society struggle without government?

choices.text: There would likely be less structure and stability
choices.feedback: Correct. The lesson explains that government provides structure, order, and stability.

choices.text: There would automatically be more fairness
choices.feedback: Incorrect. The lesson does not suggest fairness increases automatically without government.

choices.text: There would be no need for public services
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Public services are still necessary and are often organized by government.

choices.text: People would no longer need rules
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Rules are still necessary for organized society.

choices.text: Government only exists to punish people
choices.feedback: Incorrect. The lesson includes services and decision-making, not only punishment.

choices.text: Government has no effect on daily life
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Government affects daily life through laws, services, and decisions.

choices.text: Government should never make decisions for communities
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments do make decisions that affect communities.

choices.text: Government organizes society and helps communities function
choices.feedback: Correct. This reflects the central idea of the lesson.
